summary: iOS 🟥 Incorrect name and inaccessible settings after deleting device
scenario: Deleting a paired device from Poly Studio Mobile app causes incorrect device name and connection status for remaining device, blocking access to settings
description_clean: After pairing at least two devices with the app, turning off one device, selecting the disconnected device, and deleting it from the app, the user is returned to the device home screen for the remaining device. However, the name and connection status shown are incorrect (they reflect the deleted device instead of the remaining device), and the user is unable to open settings for the remaining device. Restarting the app fixes the issue. Expected behavior: Deleting a device from the app should not cause any issues for devices that remain.
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Pair at least two Bluetooth audio devices (e.g., Device A and Device B) with the app.
3. Verify both devices appear on the Home screen with correct names and connection statuses.
4. Turn off one of the paired devices (e.g., Device A).
5. In the app, navigate to the disconnected device (Device A) on the Home screen.
6. Select and delete Device A from the app (e.g., via Device Settings → Remove Device).
7. Observe the Home screen after deletion.
expected: The Home screen should display the remaining device (Device B) with its correct name and connection status. The user should be able to tap on Device B to open its Device Settings and access all features (Audio Settings, ANC, Equalizer, etc.).
actual: The Home screen shows the name and connection status of the deleted device (Device A) instead of the remaining device (Device B). The user is unable to open settings for the remaining device. Restarting the app resolves the issue.
